← All worlds/World 01
World 01 · 2 states

Healing

Films for the part of you that is quietly putting itself back together — the slow, unglamorous work of becoming whole again.

Choose an emotion
Heartbreak RecoveryEmotional Numbness
On Heartbreak Recovery

There is a particular kind of tired that follows heartbreak — not sleepy, but hollowed. The world keeps moving at its regular pace and you cannot understand why it has not noticed. You want someone to say: this is real, this is serious, you are not dramatic for how much this hurts. Cinema can do that. The right film does not rush you toward okay. It sits with you in the aching reality of having loved someone who is now a story you keep almost telling. These films were chosen because they understand the difference between closure and the slow work of becoming someone who carries this differently.

Heartbreak Recovery

Films for the long quiet after the door closed.

25 films — quick browse

🏆 Won 1 Academy Award

CS9.5
2004

Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind

Michel Gondry

View Film →

After a painful breakup, a couple turns to a clinic that erases unwanted memories — and discovers, mid-procedure, that some loves are worth the pain they leave behind.

CS9.2
2013

Her

Spike Jonze

View Film →

A lonely letter-writer in near-future Los Angeles falls in love with an AI operating system designed to learn him. A slow, devastating arc of being known.

CS8.7
2010

Blue Valentine

Derek Cianfrance

View Film →

A marriage falling apart, intercut with the same marriage falling together six years earlier. Two performances honest enough to hurt.

CS9.0
2019

Marriage Story

Noah Baumbach

View Film →

A theatre director and an actress navigate a coast-to-coast divorce, trying to remain themselves through a system designed to flatten them.

CS9.3
2004

Before Sunset

Richard Linklater

View Film →

Nine years after a night in Vienna, two not-quite-lovers meet again for an afternoon in Paris and discover what they’ve been carrying since.

CS9.2
2003

Lost in Translation

Sofia Coppola

View Film →

An ageing actor and a young photographer’s wife meet in a Tokyo hotel and form a fragile, electric friendship across one strange week.

CS8.9
2016

La La Land

Damien Chazelle

View Film →

A jazz pianist and an aspiring actress meet in Los Angeles, fall in love, and slowly build the lives that pull them apart.

CS9.1
2017

Call Me by Your Name

Luca Guadagnino

View Film →

In Northern Italy in 1983, a precocious seventeen-year-old falls in love with his father’s American summer assistant. A film about a season that becomes a life.

CS9.4
2023

Past Lives

Celine Song

View Film →

Two childhood friends in Korea drift apart across decades and continents — then reunite for a few days in New York with everything left unsaid.

CS8.4
2009

(500) Days of Summer

Marc Webb

View Film →

A greeting-card writer falls for a co-worker who keeps telling him she doesn’t believe in love. The film follows the relationship in shuffled, non-linear pieces.

CS9.6
2000

In the Mood for Love

Wong Kar-wai

View Film →

Two neighbours in 1962 Hong Kong discover their spouses are having an affair. They meet, restrain themselves, and say nothing. The restraint becomes the entire film.

CS9.2
2015

Carol

Todd Haynes

View Film →

A young Manhattan shop girl and an elegant older woman from New Jersey begin a careful, dangerous love affair in 1952 America.

CS9.5
2019

Portrait of a Lady on Fire

Céline Sciamma

View Film →

A painter is commissioned to secretly portray a young noblewoman in 18th-century Brittany who does not know she is being observed. Then she finds out.

CS9.2
2005

Brokeback Mountain

Ang Lee

View Film →

Two ranch hands in 1963 Wyoming fall in love over a summer. They return to separate lives, wives, children, and meet again for twenty years of hidden love.

CS9.0
1977

Annie Hall

Woody Allen

View Film →

Alvy Singer tries to understand why his relationship with Annie Hall ended. He tells the story directly to camera, to her, to strangers, non-linearly.

CS8.6
2004

Closer

Mike Nichols

View Film →

Four people in London form two couples, break them, reform, betray each other, and argue about honesty across several years.

CS8.7
2010

Never Let Me Go

Mark Romanek

View Film →

Three friends grow up in an English boarding school in an alternate 1990s. What they are, and what their future holds, is revealed in stages.

CS8.5
2013

Blue Is the Warmest Color

Abdellatif Kechiche

View Film →

A teenage girl in Lille discovers her identity through a consuming love affair with an older blue-haired art student. Told across several years.

CS9.1
2021

The Worst Person in the World

Joachim Trier

View Film →

A young Oslo woman spends her late twenties moving between careers, men, and ideas of herself across twelve chapters and a prologue and epilogue.

CS9.0
2011

Weekend

Andrew Haigh

View Film →

Two men in Nottingham meet in a bar on Friday, spend the weekend together, and both know it has to end on Sunday.

CS8.1
2011

Like Crazy

Drake Doremus

View Film →

A British student and an American student fall in love in LA, get separated by a visa violation, and try to maintain a relationship across years and an ocean.

CS8.3
2011

Take This Waltz

Sarah Polley

View Film →

A happily married woman in Toronto develops feelings for her neighbour across the street. The film stays with the ambivalence rather than resolving it.

CS9.1
1959

Hiroshima Mon Amour

Alain Resnais

View Film →

A French actress filming in Hiroshima has a brief affair with a Japanese architect. The present encounter unlocks a buried wartime trauma.

CS8.8
2009

A Single Man

Tom Ford

View Film →

A British professor in 1962 Los Angeles has decided today will be his last day alive, eight months after losing his partner of sixteen years.

CS8.9
2010

Certified Copy

Abbas Kiarostami

View Film →

A British author and a French antique dealer spend a day in Tuscany discussing art and authenticity. Partway through, their relationship seems to shift into something else.

Emotional Numbness →

Films for when everything is grey and you don’t know why.

Next world · 02
Loneliness →

For when no one seems to see you.

Films don't just entertain. They quietly confirm you were seen — long before anyone in your real life knew how to.

18 emotional states·277 curated films·Growing weekly
